findings.description: Eight of nine source units (email canvas width, mobile breakpoint, internal grid model, section padding, vertical rhythm/spacing scale, section top/bottom padding, element gap spacing) have no corresponding rule in the candidate set. Only the CTA-related unit (u_0008) is represented, leaving the majority of this blob's normative content unclustered/unextracted.

payload.note: add rules for canvas width (600px), mobile breakpoint (480px), internal grid model, 24px section side padding, 8px vertical rhythm base unit, 32px section top/bottom padding, and heading/paragraph/CTA/caption gap spacing

findings.description: rule_lisraya_cta_button_spec appears to bundle multiple distinct facts from u_0008 (background/text color, font, padding, min 48px touch target accessibility rule, corner radius, and a full secondary-CTA variant spec) into a single rule. The accessibility touch-target minimum and the secondary CTA styling are distinct governance/spec concerns and should be split into their own rules for correct hardness/governance tracking.

The unit explicitly labels the min 48px touch target as 'brand accessibility rule', a compliance/governance statement, but this is folded silently into a general cta_button_spec rule with no governance facet (e.g., accessibility standard reference, compliance category) attached.

Unit heading tags this CTA block as '[GENERAL, contrast-checked to palette]', signaling some facets (e.g., color choice) are general/derived guidance rather than strict brand mandates, yet the rule is uniformly marked hardness=hard_constraint/polarity=must. Hardness should be differentiated: color/font as general-derived guidance vs. touch-target/padding as hard constraints.

payload.note: color/contrast facets are general guidance; touch target and structural padding are hard constraints

findings.description: Rule polarity is 'must' but the source phrase 'One primary CTA per section maximum' expresses an upper-bound/limit constraint (must not exceed one), not a positive obligation to include a CTA. Polarity should reflect a cap/limit rather than a plain 'must'.
